session.assistant:
Wait, I see that it also renamed `PlanDataProvider` to `PrDataProvider` in the base class! That wasn't supposed to happen. Let me check what the user actually wants:
Looking back at the user's request: > 1. `RealPlanDataProvider` → `RealPrDataProvider` (defined in src/erk/tui/data/real_provider.py) > 2. `FakePlanDataProvider` → `FakePrDataProvider` (defined in tests/fakes/tests/tui_plan_data_provider.py AND tests/fakes/gateway/plan_data_provider.py — there are TWO fake classes with this name in different files)
The user only asked to rename these specific classes, not the base class `PlanDataProvider`. But actually, looking at the inheritance, if `RealPlanDataProvider(PlanDataProvider)` becomes `RealPrDataProvider`, it makes sense that it would inherit from `PrDataProvider` not `PlanDataProvider`.
Let me check if the ABC was also renamed:
session.tool_result:
diff --git a/src/erk/tui/data/provider_abc.py b/src/erk/tui/data/provider_abc.py index 2ed3908e8..c881c9655 100644 --- a/src/erk/tui/data/provider_abc.py +++ b/src/erk/tui/data/provider_abc.py @@ -2,11 +2,11 @@

Contains only the methods that produce TUI-specific types @@ -15,7 +15,7 @@ class PlanDataProvider(ABC): """
session.assistant:
